title: Death Saving Throw
excerpt: A death saving throw is a special saving throw resolved for a creature at 0 hit points that is not yet dead or stable. Repeated results determine whether that creature stabilizes or dies.
markdown:
# Death Saving Throw

A death saving throw is a special saving throw used when a creature is at 0 hit points and remains in an unstable, not-yet-dead state. Its purpose is to track whether that creature moves toward stabilization or toward death.

## Rules Use

### Crisis Resolution At 0 Hit Points
- Death saving throws apply after a creature has entered the unstable state associated with 0 hit points
- They are not ordinary defenses against an incoming effect; they resolve the creature's continuing survival state after the collapse has already happened
- A creature that is already stable or already dead is not using death saving throws in the same way

### Repeated Outcome Tracking
- Each death saving throw contributes to an accumulating success-or-failure track rather than resolving the entire situation at once
- Enough successful results can stabilize the creature
- Enough failed results can cause the creature to die

### Special Results Matter
- A natural 20 can produce an immediate swing toward recovery rather than only counting as a single ordinary success
- A natural 1 counts as a heavier setback than a normal failed result
- Damage, healing, stabilization, or other effects can interrupt or replace normal death-save progression depending on the governing rule

## Relationship To Other States

- Death saving throws exist between simple unconscious collapse and completed death
- Healing can end the crisis by moving the creature back into an active living state
- Stabilization can halt the crisis without fully restoring ordinary action-state
- If the failure track completes first, the creature becomes dead
